subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Five friends, P, Q, R, S and T are sitting in a row facing south. Q is sitting in the centre position. P is sitting at the west end. T and R are sitting on one side of Q. Two persons are sitting between P and R. Who is sitting between Q and P?
q: Five friends, P, Q, R, S and T are sitting in a row facing south. Q is sitting in the centre position. P is sitting at the west end. T and R are sitting on one side of Q. Two persons are sitting between P and R. Who is sitting between Q and P?
explanation: Arrangement facing south from west to east: P - S - Q - T - R. S is sitting between Q and P.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In a certain code language, 'ARROW' is written as 'FOOSE' and 'GERM' is written as 'THOR'. How will 'MOWER' be written in that language?
q: In a certain code language, 'ARROW' is written as 'FOOSE' and 'GERM' is written as 'THOR'. How will 'MOWER' be written in that language?
explanation: $Direct letter substitution from ARROW and GERM gives M=R, O=S, W=E, E=H, R=O ⇒ RSEHO$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Four letterclusters have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the lettercluster that is different.
q: Four letterclusters have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the lettercluster that is different.
explanation: HIJ (+1, +1), QRS (+1, +1), DEF (+1, +1) are consecutive letters. NMP is jumbled.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Eight friends, A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H, are sitting in a straight line, all facing the north. F is sitting between D and G. B is sitting between H and A. E is third to the left of G. G is sitting at one of the corners. H is third to the left of C. Who is sitting between A and E?
q: Eight friends, A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H, are sitting in a straight line, all facing the north. F is sitting between D and G. B is sitting between H and A. E is third to the left of G. G is sitting at one of the corners. H is third to the left of C. Who is sitting between A and E?
explanation: Order facing north: H - B - A - C - E - D - F - G. C is sitting between A and E.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Two different positions of the same dice marked with letters/symbols X, S, Y, &, Z and @ are shown. Select the letter/symbol that will be on the face opposite to the face having the symbol S.
q: Two different positions of the same dice marked with letters/symbols X, S, Y, &, Z and @ are shown. Select the letter/symbol that will be on the face opposite to the face having the symbol S.
explanation: From dice rotation rules, S is opposite to &.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In a certain code language, 'India is my country' means '8573', 'Sam is my friend' means '8634', 'My country' means '73' and 'Team India' means '59'. What number is the code for 'Country'?
q: In a certain code language, 'India is my country' means '8573', 'Sam is my friend' means '8634', 'My country' means '73' and 'Team India' means '59'. What number is the code for 'Country'?
explanation: $'My' = 3 (common to all sentences). In 'My country' = '73', 'Country' = 7$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: 'R+S' means 'R is the daughter of S'. 'R−S' means 'R is the husband of S'. 'R × S' means 'R is the brother of S'. If 'T × V + Z', then which of the following options is true?
q: 'R+S' means 'R is the daughter of S'. 'R−S' means 'R is the husband of S'. 'R × S' means 'R is the brother of S'. If 'T × V + Z', then which of the following options is true?

options.text: T is the uncle of Z.
T is the father of Z.
T is the son of Z.
T is the brother of Z.

explanation: T is brother of V, V is daughter of Z. Therefore, T is the son of Z.

question:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s).

Statements:
All blues are whites.
Some whites are greys.

Conclusions:
I. All greys are whites.
II. All greys are blues.
q:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s).

Statements:
All blues are whites.
Some whites are greys.

Conclusions:
I. All greys are whites.
II. All greys are blues.

options.text: Neither conclusion I nor conclusion II follows

explanation: Some whites are greys does not imply all greys are whites or blues. Neither conclusion follows.

q: In a molecule of water (H2O), what is the ratio of the mass of hydrogen to that of oxygen?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 36\sqrt{3} cm. Find its height.
q: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 36\sqrt{3} cm. Find its height.
explanation: $Side = 36\sqrt{3} / 3 = 12\sqrt{3} cm. Height = (\sqrt{3}/2) × 12\sqrt{3} = 18 cm$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A batsman scored 124 runs, which included 6 boundaries and 10 sixes. What percentage of his total score did he make by running between the wickets?
q: A batsman scored 124 runs, which included 6 boundaries and 10 sixes. What percentage of his total score did he make by running between the wickets?
explanation: $Boundary runs = 24 + 60 = 84. Running runs = 124-84 = 40. Percentage = (40 / 124) × 100 = 32 8/31 \%$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Anu is four times as good as Binni in completing a task. Together they finish the same task in 7 hours. In how many hours will Anu alone complete the task?
q: Anu is four times as good as Binni in completing a task. Together they finish the same task in 7 hours. In how many hours will Anu alone complete the task?
explanation: $Total work = (4 + 1) × 7 = 35 units. Anu's time = 35 / 4 hours$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The number of students in class IX and class X is 42 and 45, respectively. The ratio of the number of boys to girls in classes is IX and X is $9:5$ and $8:7$, respectively. What is the difference between the total number of boys and the total number of girls in both the classes taken together?
q: The number of students in class IX and class X is 42 and 45, respectively. The ratio of the number of boys to girls in classes is IX and X is 9 : 5 and 8 : 7, respectively. What is the difference between the total number of boys and the total number of girls in both the classes taken together?
explanation: $Class IX: Boys = 27, Girls = 15. Class X: Boys = 24, Girls = 21. Total Boys = 51, Total Girls = 36. Difference = 15$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: P, Q and R can complete a work in 10 days, 20 days and 30 days, respectively, working alone. How soon can the work be completed if P is assisted by Q and R on alternate days?
q: P, Q and R can complete a work in 10 days, 20 days and 30 days, respectively, working alone. How soon can the work be completed if P is assisted by Q and R on alternate days?
explanation: $Work in 2 days = (6+3) + (6+2) = 17 units out \text{ of } 60. In 6 days = 51 units. 7th day = 9 units -> Total 60 units in 7 1/17 days$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude

q: The average of 10 observations is 46. It was realised later that an observation was misread as 42 in place of 142. The correct average is:
explanation: $Increase = (142-42) / 10 = +10. Correct average = 46 + 10 = 56$.

q: A certain sum amounts to ₹9,982.50 in 2 1/2 years at 12% p.a., interest compounded 10monthly. The sum (in ₹) is:
explanation: $10month rate = 10\%, n = 3 periods. Amount = 1.331 P = 9982.50 ⇒ P = ₹7,500$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A ninedigit number 89563x87y is divisible by 72. What is the value of \\sqrt{7x - 3y}?
q: A ninedigit number 89563x87y is divisible by 72. What is the value of \\sqrt{7x - 3y}?
explanation: $For 8 divisibility: 87y ⇒ y = 2. For 9 divisibility: sum = 48 + x ⇒ x = 6. \\sqrt{7×6-3×2} = \sqrt{36} = 6$.

q: A certain sum amounts to ₹13,200 after 4 years and to ₹16,400 after 8 years at the same rate per cent p.a. at simple interest. The simple interest (in ₹) on the same sum at 10% p.a. for 3 1/5 years will be:
explanation: $4 years SI = 3200 ⇒ Principal P = ₹10,000. Interest for 3.2 years at 10\% = 10000 × 0.10 × 3.2 = ₹3,200$.

question:

Microwave ovens heat food quickly, anxiously and safely, but do not brown or bake food in the way that conventional ovens do.
q:

explanation: 'efficiently' properly describes cooking performance.

subject: English Language
question:

She left / hers umbrella / at the office.
q:
The following sentence has been divided into parts. Select the part that contains an error:
She left / hers umbrella / at the office.
explanation: Possessive adjective 'her' should modify 'umbrella' instead of pronoun 'hers'.

subject: English Language
question:
Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error:
Mohan told to / Sagar that he / was looking / very pale.
q:
Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error:
Mohan told to / Sagar that he / was looking / very pale.
explanation: 'told' takes a direct object without preposition 'to' ('Mohan told Sagar').

subject: English Language
question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
Play for time
q:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
Play for time

options.text: To do something in the stipulated time
To make excuses or do things to gain time
To play something for the sake of old times
To play music in the time provided

explanation: 'Play for time' means delay an outcome to gain an advantage.

subject: English Language
question:
Comprehension:
In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.

When the air is clear, the sunset (1)______ yellow, because the light from the sun has passed a long distance through air and the blue light (2)______ scattered away. If the air is polluted with small particles, natural (3)______ otherwise, the sunset will be more red. Sunsets over the sea may also be orange, (4)______ salt particles in the air. The sky (5)______ the sun is seen reddened, as well as the light coming directly from the sun.

SubQuestion No : 21
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.

explanation: 'appears' describes visual perception.
